title: Natural Language Processing
description: NLP fundamentals, text mining techniques, and practical examples.

sections.title: Introduction to NLP
sections.lesson:
Learning Objectives

  • Define NLP and explain why text needs preprocessing.
  • Identify common NLP tasks: sentiment analysis, topic discovery, similarity, and classification.
  • Build an end-to-end NLP workflow in Orange Data Mining using widgets.
  • Interpret results using visualizations (Word Cloud, Topic Modeling, Confusion Matrix).
  • Perform a mini-demonstration using a small dataset of customer reviews.

What is NLP?

Natural Language Processing (NLP) is a field of AI that helps computers understand, analyze, and work with human language (text or speech). In real life, NLP powers:

  • Sentiment analysis (positive/negative reviews)
  • Spam detection (filtering unwanted messages)
  • Chatbots (customer support assistants)
  • Topic discovery (what people talk about most)
  • Text similarity (recommendations / matching)

Why Text Needs Preprocessing

Computers don’t understand text the way humans do. They need text converted into numbers. But before that, we clean and standardize it so models can learn patterns.

Common Problems in Raw Text

  • Different letter cases: "Good" vs "good"
  • Punctuation/noise: "good!!!"
  • Stopwords: "the", "is", "and"
  • Word variants: "running", "runs", "run"

Common NLP Preprocessing Steps

  • Tokenization — split text into words
  • Lowercasing — standardize casing
  • Stopword removal — remove common fillers
  • Lemmatization/Stemming — reduce words to root form
  • Vectorization — transform text into numeric features (e.g., TF-IDF)

Orange Data Mining (Overview)

Orange is a visual data mining tool where you build workflows by connecting widgets (blocks). For NLP, Orange provides a Text add-on with widgets for preprocessing, sentiment analysis, topic modeling, and classification.

Tip: In Orange, NLP typically follows this pattern:
Data → Text Preprocess → Transform (TF-IDF) → Model / Visualization → Evaluation

Demonstration Dataset (Sample Reviews)

Use this sample dataset (copy into a CSV file) for your classroom demo. It contains short customer reviews with sentiment labels. 

id,review,sentiment
1,"Fast delivery and excellent packaging. Very satisfied!",positive
2,"Product arrived broken. Poor quality.",negative
3,"Customer service was helpful and fixed my issue quickly.",positive
4,"Not worth the price. Disappointed.",negative
5,"Great value for money! Will buy again.",positive
6,"Late delivery and the item is different from the photo.",negative
7,"Easy to use and works as expected.",positive
8,"Stopped working after two days. Waste of money.",negative
Note: You can also replace this with real-world data like Shopee/Lazada reviews, Facebook comments, or student feedback — as long as you have a review column.

Hands-On Demo: NLP Workflow in Orange

Step A — Install the Text Add-on

  1. Open Orange
  2. Go to Options → Add-ons
  3. Search for Text and install Orange3-Text
  4. Restart Orange

Step B — Load the Dataset

  1. Create a CSV file: reviews.csv using the sample above.
  2. In Orange Canvas, add File widget.
  3. Open reviews.csv.
  4. Check that review is recognized as Text and sentiment as Target (class label).

Step C — Build the Text Preprocessing Pipeline

Add and connect these widgets in order:

Widget Purpose Suggested Settings
File Load dataset Select CSV
Corpus (Text) Ensure text is treated as a corpus Select review as text feature
Preprocess Text Clean and normalize text Lowercase, Remove stopwords (English), Tokenize, Lemmatize (optional)
Bag of Words / TF-IDF Convert text into numeric features Use TF-IDF for better weighting
Word Cloud See frequent words visually Compare by class: positive vs negative
Checkpoint: If your TF-IDF output has many features (words), your preprocessing is working.

Common Issues and Quick Fixes

Issue Cause Fix
Text column not detected Orange treats it as string/category Use Corpus widget and select the text field
Low accuracy Too few samples / noisy text Add more documents and improve preprocessing
Topic modeling looks random Dataset is too small Use 50+ documents and reduce topics to 3–5
Word Cloud is empty Stopwords removed too aggressively Adjust stopword list and keep frequent terms

Wrap-Up

NLP is about converting human language into structured signals that models and analytics can use. With Orange Data Mining, you can rapidly prototype NLP workflows using visual blocks — great for learning and teaching core concepts like preprocessing, TF-IDF, classification, topics, and similarity.

Next Step: Try using a larger dataset (100+ reviews) and compare results between Naive Bayes, Logistic Regression, and SVM.

End of Discussion — NLP with Orange Data Mining

sections.title: Text Mining
sections.lesson:
Text Analytics, Text Mining, and Natural Language Processing

Modern organizations generate massive amounts of unstructured text data from emails, social media, reviews, reports, chat logs, and documents. To extract value from this data, techniques such as Text Analytics, Text Mining, and Natural Language Processing (NLP) are used.

Text Analytics Overview

Text Analytics refers to the process of converting unstructured text into structured data to uncover meaningful patterns, trends, and insights. It focuses on analyzing text to support decision-making and business intelligence.

  • Identifies trends and sentiments
  • Extracts key topics and keywords
  • Supports data-driven decisions
Example:
Analyzing customer reviews to determine whether feedback is positive, negative, or neutral.

Text Mining Overview

Text Mining is a subset of data mining that focuses on discovering hidden patterns and relationships within large collections of text data. It combines techniques from statistics, machine learning, and linguistics.

While text analytics focuses on interpretation and insights, text mining emphasizes pattern discovery and knowledge extraction.

Example:
Mining thousands of research papers to discover emerging topics or frequently associated terms.

Text Analytics vs Text Mining

Aspect Text Analytics Text Mining
Focus Insight and interpretation Pattern discovery
Purpose Decision support Knowledge extraction
Output Sentiment, trends, summaries Hidden patterns, associations

Natural Language Processing (NLP)

Natural Language Processing (NLP) is a field of artificial intelligence that enables computers to understand, interpret, and generate human language. NLP serves as the foundation for both text analytics and text mining.

NLP allows machines to process text similarly to how humans read and understand language.

Common NLP Tasks
  • Tokenization: The foundational process of breaking a stream of text into smaller units called "tokens" (such as words, characters, or subwords) to make the text machine-readable.
  • Part-of-speech tagging: The task of labeling each word in a sentence with its corresponding grammatical category—such as noun, verb, or adjective—based on its definition and context.
  • Named Entity Recognition (NER): The identification and classification of key information (entities) in a text into predefined categories like names of people, organizations, locations, and dates.
  • Sentiment analysis: Often called opinion mining, this involves determining the emotional tone behind a body of text to understand if the attitude expressed is positive, negative, or neutral.
  • Language translation: The automated process of converting text or speech from one language to another while ensuring the original meaning and context remain intact.
Example:
A chatbot understanding user questions and responding in natural language.

Relationship Between the Three

These concepts are closely related and often work together:

  • NLP provides language understanding techniques
  • Text Mining discovers patterns using NLP outputs
  • Text Analytics transforms results into insights for decision-making
Real-World Scenario:
Social media posts are processed using NLP → patterns are discovered using text mining → business insights are generated using text analytics.

Real-World Applications

  • Customer sentiment analysis
  • Spam and fraud detection
  • Chatbots and virtual assistants
  • Healthcare document analysis
  • Market and competitor analysis

Real-World Applications of Text Analytics, Text Mining, and NLP

Text analytics, text mining, and natural language processing (NLP) are widely used in various industries to extract meaningful insights from unstructured text data. Below are common real-world applications that demonstrate how these technologies support decision-making and automation.

Customer Reviews and Online Ratings

E-commerce platforms analyze customer reviews to understand user satisfaction and product performance.

How it works:
  • NLP interprets customer-written text
  • Text analytics classifies reviews as positive, negative, or neutral
  • Insights are summarized for business decisions
Outcome: Faster understanding of customer feedback.

Social Media Sentiment Analysis

Organizations monitor social media platforms to track public opinion about brands, products, or events.

How it works:
  • NLP processes informal language, hashtags, and emojis
  • Text mining identifies trending topics
  • Text analytics summarizes overall sentiment
Outcome: Early detection of issues and trends.

Chatbots and Virtual Assistants

Chatbots use NLP to communicate with users in natural language.

How it works:
  • NLP identifies user intent
  • System generates relevant responses
Outcome: 24/7 automated customer support.
Business Intelligence, Analytics, Data Science, and AI A Managerial Perspective (5th Edition)

sections.title: Text Mining Examples
sections.lesson:
Real-World Applications of Text Analytics, Text Mining, and NLP

Text analytics, text mining, and natural language processing (NLP) are widely used in various industries to extract meaningful insights from unstructured text data. Below are common real-world applications that demonstrate how these technologies support decision-making and automation.

1. Customer Reviews and Online Ratings

E-commerce platforms analyze customer reviews to understand user satisfaction and product performance.

How it works:
  • NLP interprets customer-written text
  • Text analytics classifies reviews as positive, negative, or neutral
  • Insights are summarized for business decisions
Outcome: Faster understanding of customer feedback.

2. Social Media Sentiment Analysis

Organizations monitor social media platforms to track public opinion about brands, products, or events.

How it works:
  • NLP processes informal language, hashtags, and emojis
  • Text mining identifies trending topics
  • Text analytics summarizes overall sentiment
Outcome: Early detection of issues and trends.

3. Chatbots and Virtual Assistants

Chatbots use NLP to communicate with users in natural language.

How it works:
  • NLP identifies user intent
  • System generates relevant responses
Outcome: 24/7 automated customer support.

4. Spam Email Detection

Email providers automatically detect and filter spam messages.

How it works:
  • Text mining finds common spam patterns
  • NLP analyzes message structure and keywords
Outcome: Cleaner and safer inboxes.

5. Document Search and Classification

Large organizations manage thousands of documents and reports using text-based systems.

How it works:
  • NLP extracts keywords and meanings
  • Text mining groups documents by topic
Outcome: Faster document retrieval and organization.

6. Healthcare Text Analysis

Hospitals analyze doctors’ notes and medical records.

How it works:
  • NLP recognizes medical terms and symptoms
  • Text mining identifies patterns across patient records
Outcome: Improved diagnosis and patient care.

7. Fraud Detection in Banking

Financial institutions analyze transaction descriptions and customer reports.

How it works:
  • Text mining detects unusual activity patterns
  • Text analytics highlights suspicious behavior
Outcome: Reduced fraud and financial risk.

8. Resume Screening and Recruitment

Companies use automated systems to evaluate job applications.

How it works:
  • NLP extracts skills and qualifications
  • Text analytics ranks candidates
Outcome: Faster and more efficient hiring.

9. Summary

Text analytics, text mining, and NLP are essential tools for transforming unstructured text into valuable insights. These technologies are widely applied across industries to automate processes, improve decisions, and enhance user experiences.

Student Activity: Real-World Text Analysis

In this activity, you will explore how Natural Language Processing (NLP), Text Mining, and Text Analytics are used to analyze real-world text data. You will analyze customer reviews and generate insights based on your findings.

Instructions

  • Form a group of 3–4 students
  • Read the sample customer reviews carefully
  • Complete all tasks below
  • Write your answers clearly and briefly

Scenario

You are helping an online store analyze customer feedback to improve product quality and customer service.

Customer Reviews:
  • “The product quality is excellent and delivery was fast.”
  • “Customer service is very slow and unhelpful.”
  • “Affordable price but the packaging was damaged.”
  • “I love the design, but the battery life is poor.”

Task 1: NLP – Understanding the Text

For each review, identify the important keywords and determine the overall sentiment.

Review Keywords Sentiment
(Positive / Negative / Neutral)
Review 1
Review 2
Review 3
Review 4

Task 2: Text Mining – Finding Patterns

Analyze all reviews together and answer the following:

  • What problems appear more than once?
  • What positive features are mentioned?

Your Answers:

  • Repeated Problems: ______________________________
  • Positive Features: ______________________________

Task 3: Text Analytics – Generating Insights

Based on your analysis, answer the questions below:

  • What is the main customer complaint?
  • What should the company improve?
  • What should the company continue doing well?

Task 4: Identify the Concept Used

Match each activity to the correct concept.

Activity Concept
Identifying keywords and sentiment NLP
Finding repeated issues in reviews Text Mining
Making business recommendations Text Analytics

Reflection

  • Why is understanding text important before making decisions?
  • How can text analytics help businesses improve?

What You Should Learn

  • How NLP helps computers understand human language
  • How text mining discovers patterns in text data
  • How text analytics supports decision-making
